rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative [3+2] annulation of 1,3-dicarbonyl indanones with styrenes/alkenes to form spirocyclic dihydrofuranones via radical intermediates
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of the 1,3-dicarbonyl (methyl 1-oxo-2,3-dihydro-1H-indene-2-carboxylate) to generate an α-carbonyl C-centered radical that adds to the alkene

key_intermediates.name: benzylic C-radical after addition to styrene/diphenylethylene
spirocyclic dihydrofuranone 3a (cyclized product incorporating 18O from H2-18O)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes the enolizable 1,3-dicarbonyl substrate 1a to an α-carbonyl radical, which adds across the alkene to give a benzylic C-radical; further single-electron oxidation at the anode yields a benzylic cation that is trapped by H2-18O / carbonyl oxygen to close the spirofuran ring.

probe_reagents.name: H2-18O
probe_reagents.observation: 18O incorporation into product 3a (and downstream 4-18O), confirming water as the oxygen source in ring closure

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: TEMPO-adduct 6 isolated in 89.6% yield from reaction of 1a with styrene + TEMPO, trapping the α-carbonyl radical (and a styrene-derived adduct 7 also observed), supporting a radical pathway

notes: Two TEMPO adducts (6 and 7) are described: 6 corresponds to trapping of the α-carbonyl radical from 1a directly, while 7 corresponds to trapping after addition to styrene — together consistent with stepwise radical addition followed by oxidation (radical-polar crossover) and water-assisted cyclization.
